201.description: One bounded batch of dead-lettered ingress deliveries re-dispatched, with remaining depth.
Instance created. The plaintext ingress secret and verifyToken are revealed once in this response — store them immediately (both masked on every later read).

204.description: Instance deleted and its session scope torn down.

200.description: Secret regenerated. The new plaintext secret is revealed once in this response; the verifyToken is also shown (unchanged).

info.title: OpenWA API
info.description: Open Source WhatsApp API Gateway - Free, Self-Hosted HTTP API

name.description: Friendly name for the API key

allowedSessions.description: Allowed session IDs this key can access

keyPrefix.description: First 8 characters of the key (for identification)

apiKey.description: Full API key (only shown once at creation)

keyPrefix.description: First 8 characters of the key (for identification)

name.description: Unique name for the session (alphanumeric and hyphens only)

config.description: Session configuration options. Set autoRejectCalls (boolean, default false) to automatically reject incoming calls — the call.received event is still emitted.

proxyUrl.description: Optional per-session egress proxy URL (http/https/socks4/socks5; credentialed form "http://user:pass@host" allowed). Must be a REAL, REACHABLE proxy — an unreachable value silently blocks the WhatsApp WebSocket (no QR is ever delivered) and the session start times out (~30s → 504 Gateway Timeout). Leave unset unless your network cannot reach WhatsApp directly.

lastError.description: Human-readable reason for the most recent terminal engine failure (only set when status is FAILED).
lastError.example: Failed to launch the browser process: spawn /usr/bin/chromium ENOENT

phoneNumber.description: Phone number to link, digits only in international format (country code + number).

pairingCode.description: The 8-character pairing code to enter in WhatsApp.

chatId.description: Chat ID in the active engine's native format (e.g. 1234567890@c.us on whatsapp-web.js)
Chat ID in the active engine's native format (e.g. 1234567890-123@g.us on whatsapp-web.js)
Chat ID, in the active engine's native format (e.g. 1234567890@c.us)

state.description: Presence to send: 'typing' or 'recording' shows the indicator; 'paused' clears it

events.description: Event types to subscribe to. '*' subscribes to all events.

secret.description: Secret key for HMAC signature verification

headers.description: Custom headers to include in webhook requests

filters.description: Optional smart pre-filter. When set, every condition must match (AND) for the webhook to fire. Omit or null to fire on every subscribed event.

retryCount.description: Number of retry attempts on failure

filters.description: Optional smart pre-filter. When set, every condition must match (AND) for the webhook to fire. Omit or null to fire on every subscribed event.

events.description: Event types to subscribe to. '*' subscribes to all events.

filters.description: Optional smart pre-filter. When set, every condition must match (AND) for the webhook to fire. Omit or null to fire on every subscribed event.

chatId.description: WhatsApp chat ID (phone@c.us for individual, groupId@g.us for groups)

mentions.description: WIDs to @mention (e.g. ["62811@c.us"]). The text/caption must also contain the @<number> token.

messageId.description: The message id, assigned when the gateway accepts the message for sending. A 201 here means the message was handed to the WhatsApp client — it does NOT confirm delivery. WhatsApp does not reject an unregistered recipient synchronously, so a message to a number that is not on WhatsApp still returns 201 with a valid messageId; whether it later delivers, stalls, or is reported as an error reaches you asynchronously, if at all. To confirm a number is on WhatsApp before sending, use GET /api/sessions/{sessionId}/contacts/check/{number}; track real delivery via the message `status` field (sent → delivered → read, or failed if WhatsApp reports an error for it). A message resting at `sent` is not diagnostic on its own: a registered recipient whose device has not come online since the send stays at `sent` too.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) at which the gateway accepted the message for sending.

chatId.description: WhatsApp chat ID (phone@c.us for individual, groupId@g.us for groups)

templateId.description: Template ID to render. Provide either templateId or templateName.

templateName.description: Template name to render. Provide either templateId or templateName.

vars.description: Variables substituted into {{placeholder}} tokens in the template

mimetype.description: Media MIME type (required when using base64)

mentions.description: WIDs to @mention (e.g. ["62811@c.us"]). The text/caption must also contain the @<number> token.

mimetype.description: Media MIME type (required when using base64)

mentions.description: WIDs to @mention (e.g. ["62811@c.us"]). The text/caption must also contain the @<number> token.

ptt.description: Send as a WhatsApp voice note (PTT — mic bubble + waveform). Provide audio/ogg; codecs=opus bytes for reliable playback; when the mimetype is omitted it defaults to that for voice notes. Expects a JSON boolean. Default false = plain audio file. Only valid on send-audio.

options.description: Options to vote on (WhatsApp allows between 2 and 12)

allowMultipleAnswers.description: Allow voters to pick several options (default single choice)

emoji.description: Emoji to react with. Send an empty string to remove the reaction.

body.description: New text body for the message

ptt.description: Audio only: send as a WhatsApp voice note (PTT)

delayBetweenMessages.description: Delay between messages in ms (min: 1000, default: 3000)

batchId.description: Custom batch ID (auto-generated if not provided)

name.description: Unique template name within the session

header.description: Optional header text, prepended to the rendered body

footer.description: Optional footer text, appended to the rendered body

inviteCode.description: Group invite code (the token from a https://chat.whatsapp.com/<code> link)

announce.description: Only admins can send messages (announce group)

locked.description: Only admins can edit group info (locked group)

ephemeralSeconds.description: Disappearing-messages timer in seconds; 0 disables. Known values: 86400 (24h), 604800 (7d), 7776000 (90d)

description.description: New group description (may be empty to clear it)

status.description: New about/status text (may be empty to clear it; WhatsApp limit: 139 characters)

mimetype.description: Image MIME type (required when using base64)

recipients.description: Recipient JIDs (1–256). WhatsApp Status is not posted to a group — use @c.us or @lid individuals. Honored on the Baileys engine only: whatsapp-web.js ignores this allow-list and broadcasts to the account's status-privacy audience.

url.description: Public http(s) URL of the media (server-fetched, SSRF-guarded).

mimetype.description: MIME type. Required when sending base64.

recipients.description: Recipient JIDs (1–256), @c.us or @lid. Honored on the Baileys engine only: whatsapp-web.js ignores this allow-list and broadcasts to the account's status-privacy audience.
Recipient JIDs (1–256), @c.us or @lid. Honored on the Baileys engine only: whatsapp-web.js ignores this allow-list and broadcasts to the account's status-privacy audience.

chatId.description: Chat to send the product card to (@c.us or @g.us).

body.description: Optional body text accompanying the product card.

chatId.description: Chat to send the catalog to (@c.us or @g.us).

body.description: Optional body text accompanying the catalog.

url.description: HTTP(S) URL of the plugin .zip to download and install

sessions.description: Sessions to activate the plugin for; ['*'] = all numbers, [] = none

builtIn.description: Whether this is a built-in plugin

ingressCapable.description: Whether this plugin can host provisioned ingress instances

sessionScoped.description: Whether the plugin is scoped to specific sessions (false = global)

activeSessions.description: Sessions this plugin is activated for; ['*'] = all numbers

configUi.description: Sandboxed-iframe config editor (entry HTML + optional height)

i18n.description: Localized dashboard text (name/description/config titles) per locale code

sessionConfig.description: Per-session config overrides, keyed by sessionId (secrets redacted)

error.description: Error message if plugin is in error state

instanceId.description: Operator-chosen instance id (unique within the plugin). Namespaces the ingress URL and the instance secret.

sessionScope.description: Session id the instance is scoped to. Omit for all sessions.

verifyToken.description: Token echoed back for the provider webhook verification handshake. Auto-generated when omitted.

secret.description: Ingress HMAC secret shared with the provider. Omit to auto-generate a random 64-hex secret. Masked (****) on every read.

config.description: Per-instance config slice passed to the adapter (shape defined by the plugin).

route.description: Plugin-declared route segment the provider posts to.

url.description: Full ingress URL (BASE_URL + plugin/instance/route), or a relative path when BASE_URL is unset.

pluginId.description: Plugin id this instance belongs to.

instanceId.description: Operator-chosen instance id (unique within the plugin).

sessionScope.description: Session id the instance is scoped to, or null for all sessions.

secret.description: Ingress HMAC secret. Masked ('***') on every read; plaintext returned only once on create/regenerate-secret.

verifyToken.description: Provider verify-token. Masked ('***') on reads when set; plaintext on create/regenerate-secret.

config.description: Per-instance config slice passed to the adapter, or null.

enabled.description: Whether ingress is accepted and dispatch is active.

ingressUrls.description: Ingress URLs the provider posts webhook deliveries to.

enabled.description: Whether the instance is enabled (ingress accepted, dispatch active).

sessionScope.description: Session id the instance is scoped to. Omit for all sessions.

config.description: Per-instance config slice passed to the adapter.
